message.content:
You are verifier #2, independent of all other verifiers. Do NOT confirm findings optimistically — only confirm if you can independently verify the issue exists in the actual code at /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay.

Finding F-008: "versioner.put() failure raises raw untyped exception instead of IngestionError"
File: src/assay_engine/pipeline.py (line 368)
Description: versioner.put(str(plan.source)) at line 368 is called without error handling. A storage failure propagates as a raw untyped exception, not IngestionError as documented.
PoC: Pass LocalDataVersioner pointing at read-only directory. Call run_study. Raises PermissionError with no IngestionError wrapping and no trail entry.

Instructions:
1. Read the actual file at /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py
2. Look at the code at line 368 and surrounding context
3. Independently verify whether the described problem EXISTS in the current code
4. Check if the PoC is reproducible from the actual code as written
5. Consider whether this is already fixed (pass-1 #101-#122, pass-2 #124-#156 fixed many issues)
6. Return your independent verdict

Confirmed = true ONLY if: (a) the code problem exists as described, (b) the PoC is reproducible from current code, (c) it is a genuine production-readiness concern. Set confirmed=false for speculative issues, issues already fixed, or issues where the PoC does not match what the code actually does.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/persistence/versioning.py:26:    def put(self, path: str) -> str:
/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/persistence/versioning.py:43:class LocalDataVersioner:
/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/persistence/versioning.py:60:    def put(self, path: str) -> str:

1	"""Data versioning — content-addressed, local, deterministic (METHODOLOGY.md §7, ADR-0003).
2	
3	Every finding must cite the exact bytes it was computed from. This provides a dependency-free,
4	on-box content-addressing store: an artifact is hashed (SHA-256) and copied under a local
5	store keyed by that hash, so the same bytes always yield the same version id and any artifact
6	can be re-fetched by id. (The prior platform used DVC via an external tool; this keeps the
7	engine self-contained — an adapter may swap in DVC by implementing the same Protocol.)
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import hashlib
13	import os
14	import re
15	import shutil
16	import tempfile
17	from pathlib import Path
18	from typing import Protocol
19	
20	_CHUNK = 1 << 20  # 1 MiB
21	_SHA256_HEX = re.compile(r"[0-9a-f]{64}")
22	
23	
24	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
25	class DataVersioner(Protocol):
26	    def put(self, path: str) -> str:
27	        """Version the artifact at ``path``; return its content hash / version id."""
28	        ...
29	
30	    def fingerprint(self, path: str) -> str:
31	        """Return the content hash of ``path`` without storing it."""
32	        ...
33	
34	
35	def _hash_file(path: Path) -> str:
36	    h = hashlib.sha256()
37	    with path.open("rb") as f:
38	        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(_CHUNK), b""):
39	            h.update(chunk)
40	    return h.hexdigest()
41	
42	
43	class LocalDataVersioner:
44	    """Content-addressed local artifact store. Deterministic and fully offline.
45	
46	    ``store_dir`` defaults to ``ASSAY_DATA_STORE`` or ``./.assay-data``. Files are stored under
47	    ``<store>/<aa>/<full-hash>`` (sharded by the first two hex chars).
48	    """
49	
50	    def __init__(self, store_dir: str | None = None) -> None:
51	        root = store_dir or os.environ.get("ASSAY_DATA_STORE") or str(Path.cwd() / ".assay-data")
52	        self._root = Path(root)
53	
54	    def fingerprint(self, path: str) -> str:
55	        p = Path(path)
56	        if not p.is_file():
57	            raise FileNotFoundError(f"cannot version a non-file path: {path!r}")
58	        return _hash_file(p)
59	
60	    def put(self, path: str) -> str:
61	        """Store the artifact under its content hash; return the hash (version id).
62	
63	        Idempotent: storing the same bytes twice is a no-op returning the same id. Publish is
64	        atomic — content is copied to a *per-writer-unique* temp file (so two processes
65	        storing the same digest never clobber a shared temp) and then ``os.replace``-d into
66	        place, so a reader never observes a partial artifact. The store is content-addressed,
67	        so an interrupted/duplicate write is harmless.
68	        """
69	        digest = self.fingerprint(path)
70	        dest = self._root / digest[:2] / digest
71	        if dest.exists():
72	            return digest
73	        dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
74	        fd, tmp_name = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=str(dest.parent), prefix=".tmp-")
75	        os.close(fd)
76	        tmp = Path(tmp_name)
77	        try:
78	            shutil.copy2(path, tmp)
79	            os.replace(tmp, dest)  # atomic publish within the same filesystem
80	        finally:
81	            tmp.unlink(missing_ok=True)  # no-op once replaced; cleans up on copy failure
82	        return digest
83	
84	    def path_for(self, digest: str) -> Path:
85	        """Local path of a previously-stored artifact by its version id.
86	
87	        The version id MUST be a SHA-256 hex digest (the only form :meth:`put` ever returns).
88	        Validating it rejects path-traversal payloads (e.g. ``../../etc/passwd``) that would
89	        otherwise compose a path outside the store (#112).
90	        """
91	        if not _SHA256_HEX.fullmatch(digest):
92	            raise ValueError(
93	                f"invalid version id {digest!r} (expected a 64-char sha256 hex digest)"
94	            )
95	        return self._root / digest[:2] / digest
96

content.text:
The evidence is clear. The `try/except` block at lines 348-352 covers only `defn.parser.parse()` and `defn.parser.source_fingerprint()`. The call to `versioner.put()` at line 368 is **outside** that try/except block — it sits in the body of the `with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):` block but after the try/except has closed. There is no wrapping try/except around line 368 that would catch and convert storage failures to `IngestionError`.

The `LocalDataVersioner.put()` can raise:
- `FileNotFoundError` (from `self.fingerprint()` at line 57 if the path is not a file)
- `PermissionError` (from `dest.parent.mkdir()`, `tempfile.mkstemp()`, `shutil.copy2()`, or `os.replace()` if the store dir is read-only)
- `OSError` in general

None of these would be wrapped as `IngestionError`. The `trail.record()` at line 369 would also be skipped since the exception propagates before it runs.

The finding is confirmed as described.

input.reasoning: The code at line 368 in /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py calls `versioner.put(str(plan.source))` with no error handling. The try/except block that wraps parser calls (lines 348-352) ends before line 368 — the versioner call is outside it. LocalDataVersioner.put() (versioning.py lines 60-82) can raise FileNotFoundError (from fingerprint() if path is not a file), PermissionError, or OSError from mkdir/mkstemp/copy2/os.replace when the store directory is read-only. None of these are caught and re-raised as IngestionError. The documented contract ("Raises IngestionError on a bad source") is violated. Additionally, the trail.record("ingest", ...) at line 369 is also skipped when versioner.put() raises, so no audit trail entry is written. The PoC (LocalDataVersioner pointing at a read-only directory, then calling run_study) would produce a raw PermissionError with no IngestionError wrapping and no trail entry, exactly as described. This is not fixed in any prior hardening pass based on the code as it stands.
